palette_hint: off-white background (#f5f2ec), near-black text (#1a1a1a), deep red accent (#b5001f), secondary steel-blue accent (#2a4a6b) for section labels and links
type_style: Georgia serif for headlines and body text, system sans-serif (Arial/Helvetica) for labels, bylines, and navigation; heavy weight on headlines, normal weight body
description: Ironshore Dispatch is a fictional independent news magazine covering global affairs, data journalism, and opinion, styled after a classic broadsheet-meets-digital publication.
constraints: persistent top masthead (~64px tall) with publication name in large serif, horizontal primary nav below it, sticky to top on scroll
front-page uses a three-column layout: wide center column (~50%) flanked by two narrower side columns (~25% each), with a full-width hero story strip above
section-label tags (e.g. WORLD, OPINION) rendered as small all-caps sans-serif text in deep red (#b5001f) above every headline
right sidebar (~260px) appears on world, opinion, and archive pages showing 'Most Read' list and a newsletter signup block
data-desk page uses a two-column layout with a sticky left filter panel (~220px) and a scrollable right area containing a dense HTML table with sortable-looking column headers

page_specs.front-page: Full-width hero strip at top features the lead story with a large colored placeholder rectangle (dark gray), bold headline, and two-sentence summary. Below, a three-column grid shows six secondary stories each with a small colored placeholder, section tag, headline, and byline. Bottom of page has a full-width 'Latest Updates' ticker-style row listing five short headlines separated by dividers.
page_specs.world: Two-column layout: main content area on the left (~calc(100% - 260px)) lists five long-form article previews each with a horizontal rule separator, section tag, headline, 3-line excerpt, byline, and date. Right sidebar shows a 'Most Read' numbered list of 7 headlines and a 'Newsletter' signup block with an email input and a button styled in steel-blue.
page_specs.data-desk: Sticky left filter panel lists checkboxes for six topic filters (Economy, Climate, Elections, Trade, Health, Migration) and a date-range pair of text inputs. The scrollable right area has a page title 'Data Desk', a one-paragraph methodology note, and a dense HTML table with 12 rows and 6 columns (Country, Year, Metric, Value, Change, Source) with alternating row shading and bold column headers that visually suggest sorting controls.
page_specs.opinion: Three-column layout: left column (~200px) shows a vertical 'Columnists' list with five author names and one-line bios. Center column is the main reading area with two full opinion pieces shown — each with section tag, headline, author name in bold sans-serif, publish date, and four paragraphs of concrete text. Right sidebar mirrors the world page with 'Most Read' and newsletter blocks.
page_specs.archive: Top of page has a horizontal filter bar with a text search input, a month/year dropdown, and five section-toggle buttons (All, World, Data, Opinion, Culture). Below, a two-column masonry-like grid of 10 archive story cards each containing a section tag, headline, short excerpt, and date. Right sidebar shows a 'Browse by Month' block listing the past eight months as links and a 'Topics A–Z' compact list.
